sentence: Here is the SOAP note based on the transcript:
Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Jacqueline, is a 30-year-old woman who presents to the office with anxiety about her recent blood work showing a diagnosis of hepatitis C, which she was not previously aware of.
She reports feeling fine and attributes her fatigue to working long hours.
She has a history of IV drug use in the past, but has been clean for over 15 years.
She also reports smoking 1-2 cigarettes per day and drinking about 1 beer per day on average.
Past medical history: The patient has a family history of hypertension, diabetes, and depression.
She has not mentioned any other medical conditions.
Review of systems: The patient reports feeling tired at the end of the day, but denies any other symptoms.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient appears in no distress and no jaundice is noted on skin exam.
Heart exam reveals a 2/6 systolic murmur, which has not changed from last year.
Lung exam is clear with no wheezes, rales, or rhonchi.
Abdominal exam is soft with normal bowel sounds and no hepatosplenomegaly.
Musculoskeletal exam is normal with no gait disturbance or edema.
Test Results: The patient's recent blood work shows a positive HCV antibody test and an elevated AST of 39, but normal albumin and total bilirubin levels.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a confirmed diagnosis of hepatitis C, a viral infection that affects the liver.
Problem list: Hepatitis C; active; confirmed
Plan:
I prescribed nothing at this time, as the patient requires additional blood work to confirm the diagnosis and determine the severity of her liver disease.
I ordered a special ultrasound to check for fibrosis of the liver.
I also advised the patient to have her family members undergo the same screening.
I will see the patient in three weeks to review the results of the additional testing.
Follow-up: I will see the patient in three weeks to review the results of the additional testing.
